WFM Team · Module 1 of 9

Getting Started
with Claude

Your self-paced guide to using AI in your daily WFM work. Work through each module at your own pace, practice with real scenarios, and get instant feedback on your prompts.

9
Modules
~45
Minutes
4
Practice Exercises

What is Claude?

Claude is an AI assistant made by Anthropic. Unlike a search engine, Claude reads your full context, understands what you are trying to do, and produces structured output you can use immediately — emails, reports, schedules, analyses, and working tools.

For our WFM team, Claude is most useful as a drafting partner, a data interpreter, and a tool builder. It handles the time-consuming parts so you can focus on decisions.

📄

Reads & Understands

Upload Excel files, PDFs, or paste raw data. Claude reads context and gives you structured insights instantly.

✉️

Writes & Communicates

Emails, incident reports, proposals, coaching notes — drafted in your professional voice.

🔧

Builds & Automates

Creates tools, dashboards, and reports you can actually use — no coding skills required.

How to access Claude

Go to claude.ai in any browser. Create a free account using your email. The free plan handles most daily WFM tasks.

Free vs Pro: The free account supports text and file uploads. Pro unlocks larger files, longer conversations, and priority access — useful for heavy use like large Excel analysis.

One important thing before you start

Claude does not remember previous conversations. Every new tab is a fresh start. This is why setting your preferences (Module 4) is so important — it gives Claude a consistent starting point every time.

Module 2 of 9

Real tools built for this team

📦 What We Built
⏱ 4 min

Built through conversations — not by a developer

Every tool below was built by describing what was needed in plain language, letting Claude build a first version, then refining from there. Most were production-ready in one or two conversations.

REPORTING
Agent Metrics Dashboard
Live Google Sheets data, 6 tabs, KPIs, auto-insights, multi-select filters
SCHEDULING
Break Schedule Generator
PH Labor Code compliant, 15-60-15 and 30-30-30, swimlane and heatmap views
TRAINING
Training Pull-Out Scheduler
Shift-aware auto-assignment, batch scheduling, Excel export
PLATFORM
WFM Tool Hub (GitHub Pages)
Dark landing page linking all tools — EOD reports, schedulers, onboarding
OPERATIONS
Incident Report Generator
AI-assisted, A4 preview, editable fields, PDF and Excel export
INNOVATION
Amazon Connect AI POC Deck
Proposal and PowerPoint for call deflection using inbound self-service AI

The pattern

The Agent Metrics Dashboard began with: "I need a dashboard that reads from Google Sheets and shows agent performance by day." The Break Schedule Generator started with: "Build a break scheduler that follows Philippine Labor Code."

Key insight: Describe what you need in plain language. Claude builds a first version. You refine it by saying what to change. This is not a special skill — it is just clear communication.

How your role can use Claude

👤 Role-Based Use Cases
⏱ 4 min

Pick the use case closest to your daily work

📅

WFM / Scheduling

Generate break schedules · Build training batches · Analyze headcount vs. budget · Draft staffing proposals

📊

Reporting & Ops

Auto-format EOD reports · Interpret AHT, SLA, OB data · Write incident escalations · Create dashboards from Excel

👥

Leadership & Comms

Draft professional emails · Write coaching feedback · Build executive summaries · Prepare Post-Day Review reports

These are not hypotheticals

Every item in those lists has already been done on this team using Claude. Pick the one most relevant to your role — that is where you start your practice exercises.

Your starting point: Think of the last thing you had to write or produce at work that took longer than it should have. That is your first Claude task.

Module 3 of 9
Module 4 of 9

Setting your preferences

⚙️ Profile Setup
⏱ 5 min + exercise

Tell Claude who you are — once

Preferences are instructions you save in your Claude account. Once set, Claude applies them to every response automatically — no need to re-explain your role, format preference, or focus areas.

🧠

Who You Are

Your job title, company, team, and level of expertise. This gives Claude context before you type a single word.

💬

How You Want Answers

Bullet points or paragraphs? Concise or detailed? Formal or conversational? Claude will match your style.

🔍

Your Focus Areas

Topics Claude should assume are relevant: scheduling, AHT, SLA, adherence, contact center operations.

Where to set it

In claude.ai: click your profile icon → Settings → Profile → paste your preferences text → save.

Example you can adapt:

"I am a WFM team member at Detector Inspector Philippines working in a contact center. I manage scheduling, reporting, and operations. Always respond in bullet points unless I ask for something else. Be concise and professional. Focus on WFM topics: AHT, SLA, adherence, overbreak, aux utilization, scheduling, and contact center operations."

Practice: Write your own preferences

Write your own preference prompt below. Include your role, how you want answers formatted, and the WFM topics most relevant to your work. Claude will evaluate and suggest improvements.

claude.ai › Settings › Profile
Exercise 1

Your task: Write a preferences prompt for your Claude account. Tell Claude who you are, how you want answers formatted, and what WFM topics matter most.

Include at minimum: your job title, preferred output format, and 2–3 WFM focus topics.

📝 Complete this exercise to unlock the next module — write your prompt and click Check My Prompt.
Your Preference Prompt
Checks your prompt for Identity · Format · Focus · Clarity

Module 4 of 9
Module 5 of 9

The 4-part prompt framework

✏️ Core Skill
⏱ 6 min + exercise

The quality of your output depends on the quality of your input

Claude produces better results when your prompt includes four things. You do not always need all four — but when a response misses the mark, the fix is almost always one of these.

01

Context

Who you are and what situation you are in.

"I am a WFM Supervisor at a contact center in the Philippines..."
02

Task

What you specifically need — be as precise as possible.

"Write an escalation email to IT about a network outage that affected 80 agents."
03

Format

How you want the answer presented.

"Give me a bullet list / table / one paragraph summary."
04

Tone

The style and voice that fits your use case.

"Professional but direct. Keep it under 150 words."

Claude refines as you go

If the first output is not right, stay in the same thread. Say: "Make it more concise", "Add a table", or "Change the tone to be more direct." Claude holds the full conversation context.

Pro tip: A follow-up in the same thread is often more powerful than a brand new prompt. The second message builds on everything Claude already knows.

Practice: Rewrite a weak prompt

Rewrite the weak prompt using all four framework parts. The team data is provided — add context, specify the task, choose a format, and set the tone.

claude.ai › New conversation
Exercise 2

Weak prompt to improve: "Tell me about my team." — Rewrite this using Context, Task, Format, and Tone for a Post-Day Review Meeting summary.

Team data: Anna AHT 6.2m, OB 18m, Adherence 88% | Ben AHT 4.8m, OB 32m, Adherence 74% | Cara AHT 5.1m, OB 12m, Adherence 95%

📝 Complete this exercise to unlock the next module — apply the 4-part framework and click Check My Prompt.
Your Improved Prompt
Checks Context · Task · Format · Tone separately

Module 5 of 9
Module 6 of 9

Strong vs weak prompts

🔍 Examples
⏱ 5 min + exercise

See the difference a structured prompt makes

✓ Strong Prompt
✗ Weak Prompt
I am a WFM Supervisor in a PH contact center. Write a professional email to IT escalating a 30-min internet outage at 3:30 PM that caused 80 agents to lose access. Include a request for an RCA report within 24 hours.
Write an email about the internet problem.
I have this agent data: [paste table]. Summarize their key performance issues in bullet points I can use for a Post-Day Review Meeting. Focus on overbreak and aux utilization.
What should I say to a low performer?
Create a 2-week break schedule for 12 agents on 9-hour shifts (8AM–5PM). Use 15-60-15 format. No two agents on break at the same time. Output as a table.
Make a break schedule.

What makes a prompt strong

Every strong prompt has the same pattern: specific role as context, a concrete and scoped task, a stated output format, and an explicit tone. The weak prompts have none of these.

The test: Before sending, ask — could someone else read this and know exactly what output I need? If not, add more specifics.

Practice: Performance summary prompt

Your supervisor needs a quick update for today's Post-Day Review Meeting. Write a prompt that produces a usable summary from the agent data.

claude.ai › New conversation
Exercise 3

Scenario: You have 5 minutes before the Post-Day Review Meeting. Write a prompt that produces a ready-to-use performance update.

Data: Anna AHT 6.2m, OB 18m, Adherence 88%, Prod Aux 72% | Ben AHT 4.8m, OB 32m, Adherence 74%, Prod Aux 58% | Cara AHT 5.1m, OB 12m, Adherence 95%, Prod Aux 81% | OB threshold: 15m | SLA: 90%

📝 Complete this exercise to unlock the next module — write a strong performance summary prompt and click Check My Prompt.
Your Performance Summary Prompt
Include the data, your role, and expected output format

Module 6 of 9
Module 7 of 9

Know what Claude can and cannot do

⚡ Capabilities
⏱ 4 min

Set the right expectations

Claude Can

  • Read and analyze uploaded files (Excel, PDF, CSV)
  • Write emails, reports, proposals, coaching notes
  • Build single-file HTML tools and dashboards
  • Explain data and suggest data-driven decisions
  • Refine and iterate based on your feedback
  • Understand WFM terms (AHT, SLA, Occupancy)

Limitations to Know

  • Does not retain memory across different conversations
  • Cannot access internal systems unless you paste the data
  • May make errors — always verify critical outputs
  • Knowledge has a cutoff date for recent events
  • Does not replace human judgment on sensitive decisions
  • Avoid sharing sensitive personal data or passwords

The memory point deserves extra attention

Every time you open a new conversation tab, Claude starts completely fresh. It has no idea what you discussed yesterday or in a different tab.

What to do: Keep related tasks in the same thread. Set your preferences in your profile so Claude always has context from the start — without you having to re-explain your role every time.

Module 7 of 9
Module 8 of 9

How long do tasks actually take?

⏱ Time Estimates
⏱ 3 min

Real numbers from our own builds

These estimates are from actual work done by this team using Claude. The first few times will take longer as you get comfortable. After a week of regular use, Basic tasks feel almost instant.

TaskEst. TimeLevel
Draft a professional email (escalation, request, policy)30 secondsBasic
Write an incident report or executive summary1–2 minutesBasic
Analyze an uploaded Excel file and give insights2–3 minutesIntermediate
Build a formatted report from raw data or notes5–10 minutesIntermediate
Create a PowerPoint deck with content10–15 minutesAdvanced
Build a single-file HTML tool (scheduler, dashboard)30–60 minutesAdvanced
Build an Agent Metrics Dashboard (full, live data)2–4 hours (iterative)Expert

The learning curve is short

Most team members reach comfortable speed with Basic tasks within 2–3 days of regular use. The key is using Claude for real tasks every day — not just practicing in isolation.

Recommendation: Start with one Basic task per day this week. Replace one email or report you would normally write from scratch. By Friday it will feel natural.

Module 8 of 9
Module 9 of 9

Your first week plan

🚀 Get Started
⏱ 4 min + final exercise

Three things to do this week

🚀

1. Try it for a real task

Draft your next email or escalation using Claude. Compare the output to what you would normally write. Edit it to your voice.

⚙️

2. Practice the framework

Use the 4-part structure every time: Context, Task, Format, Tone. The more specific you are, the faster you get what you need.

✨

3. Access at claude.ai

Start with a free account. For heavy use, the Pro plan unlocks larger file uploads and longer conversation contexts.

Final exercise: SLA Risk Flag

SLA risk is a shared WFM responsibility — everyone's concern on the floor. Use the agent data to write a prompt that identifies SLA risk contributors and drafts a short ops alert.

claude.ai › New conversation
Final Exercise

Scenario: SLA is at risk. Identify contributing agents and draft a short ops alert for the floor — urgent but factual in tone.

Data: Anna AHT 6.2m, OB 18m, Adherence 88% | Ben AHT 4.8m, OB 32m, Adherence 74% | Cara AHT 5.1m, OB 12m, Adherence 95% | OB threshold: 15m | SLA: 90% | Shift: 8AM–5PM

📝 Complete this final exercise to unlock your certificate — write a strong SLA risk alert prompt and click Check My Prompt.
Your SLA Risk Alert Prompt
After evaluation, try: "Rewrite as a 2-sentence group chat message"

You have completed the training

You now have the foundation. Claude is set up, you know the four-part prompt framework, you have practiced with real WFM data, and you have a first-week plan.

The most important thing now: Use it regularly. The more you use it, the faster you get at prompting, and the more time you reclaim every day. Questions? Just ask Claude: "How do I use you for workforce management?"
